description: Fix batch --chain advancing to downstream PRDs when a loop exhausts its iteration budget (or hits a deadline) with todo/in_progress work remaining. Two-layer defense: (1) reconcile_ambiguous_exit in orchestrator post-loop sets exit_code=1 when budget/deadline ends with active tasks; (2) LoopResult.prd_complete + batch chain gate stops chain on !prd_complete even if exit code drifts. Design: docs/designs/batch-chain-incomplete-stop.md (approved 2026-06-12).

criteria: Rust: No warnings in cargo check output
Rust: No warnings in cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings output
Scoped tests for touched modules pass (cargo test reconcile_ambiguous_exit, batch::tests, engine::tests as applicable)
chain=false batch behavior unchanged — no validation, tracking, or stop-on-incomplete paths touched
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — Reuse count_remaining_active_tasks / classify_drained_queue predicate; do not invent a second incompleteness check
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — reconcile runs ONLY on outer post-while path, BEFORE step 17.5 task resets
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — chain=false and was_stopped paths byte-identical except where design requires
6. POLISH — --chain help text mentions iteration-budget exhaustion
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding unit test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001
chain=false must remain a complete no-op — zero new code paths when chain is off

prohibitedOutcomes: Calling reconcile_ambiguous_exit inside per-wave terminals, drained short-circuits, merge-halts, or signal paths — only the outer post-while fallthrough
Calling reconcile AFTER step 17.5/17.6 task resets — would erase todo/in_progress evidence of incompleteness
Downgrading exit_code when already nonzero (crashes, blocked=2, stale abort, merge-halt)
Changing was_stopped exit 0 semantics — intentional .stop stays clean exit even with remaining work
Surfacing prd_complete in batch summary or PrdRunResult in this change — out of scope
Full e2e batch run spawning real agent loops — unit/structure tests only
Populating synergyWith / batchWith / conflictsWith — ignored by task-mgr next
Per-task or top-level model fields — config-driven routing only
Tests that only assert 'no crash' without verifying exit_code / prd_complete / chain skip structure

acceptanceCriteria: Function `reconcile_ambiguous_exit` exists in wave_scheduler.rs as pub(crate) with signature taking conn, task_prefix, was_stopped, and &mut exit_code / exit_reason / final_run_status
Function calls count_remaining_active_tasks exactly once per invocation

Unit test reconcile_ambiguous_exit_max_iter_with_todo_sets_exit_1: seed todo task under prefix, pass exit_code=0 and reason max iterations reached, was_stopped=false → exit_code becomes 1 and reason contains task(s) remaining

Unit test reconcile_ambiguous_exit_max_iter_drained_sets_complete: all tasks done/irrelevant/skipped/blocked, reason max iterations reached → exit_code stays 0, reason becomes all tasks complete, final_run_status Completed
Unit test reconcile_ambiguous_exit_was_stopped_unchanged: todo task present, was_stopped=true → exit_code and reason unchanged
Unit test reconcile_ambiguous_exit_deadline_with_remaining: reason deadline reached, todo present → exit_code 1
orchestrator.rs calls reconcile_ambiguous_exit once on the outer post-while path before last_claimed_task reset (grep-verified ordering)
Negative: reconcile is NOT called inside the should_stop / terminal match block or inside run_wave_iteration handling
Known-bad: if reconcile runs after reset_task_to_todo, the max_iter_with_todo test would fail to set exit 1 — placement before step 17.5 is mandatory
Failure mode: count_remaining_active_tasks query failure returns 0 per existing helper — reconcile treats as drained (documented existing behavior, do not change helper)
userStories.description:
Implement Layer 1: honest loop exit code when iteration ceiling or deadline is hit with active work remaining.

DO:
- Add `pub(crate) fn reconcile_ambiguous_exit(conn: &Connection, task_prefix: Option<&str>, was_stopped: bool, exit_code: &mut i32, exit_reason: &mut String, final_run_status: &mut RunStatus)` in `src/loop_engine/wave_scheduler.rs` immediately after `classify_drained_queue` (same module as `count_remaining_active_tasks`).
- Read `count_remaining_active_tasks(conn, task_prefix)` ONCE; branch on the count.
- Decision table:
  - `was_stopped == true` → return immediately (no mutation)
  - `*exit_code != 0` → return immediately (do not downgrade)
  - `exit_reason` is exactly `"max iterations reached"` or `"deadline reached"` AND count > 0 → `*exit_code = 1`, `*exit_reason = format!("{} with {} task(s) remaining", ...)` , `*final_run_status = RunStatus::Aborted`
  - `exit_reason == "max iterations reached"` AND count == 0 → `*exit_reason = "all tasks complete".to_string()`, `*final_run_status = RunStatus::Completed` (exit stays 0)
  - Deadline + count == 0: leave `exit_reason = "deadline reached"` (exit 0) — add one-line comment that symmetric upgrade is optional polish
- Call reconcile ONLY in `src/loop_engine/orchestrator.rs` immediately after the `while iteration < max_iterations` loop ends (line ~672), BEFORE step 17.5 `reset_task_to_todo` for `last_claimed_task`.
- Import and call from orchestrator; pass `&conn`, `task_prefix.as_deref()`, `was_stopped`, `&mut exit_code`, `&mut exit_reason`, `&mut final_run_status`.

DO NOT:
- Call reconcile from wave terminal paths, `classify_drained_queue` short-circuits, merge-halt, stale abort, or `should_stop` match arms
- Call reconcile after step 17.5/17.6 resets
- Add a second SQL incompleteness predicate — reuse `count_remaining_active_tasks`

Edge cases:
- was_stopped with remaining todo tasks → exit 0 unchanged
- exit_code already 2 (blocked) → unchanged
- max iterations + all tasks terminal → upgrade to all tasks complete
- deadline + todo remaining → exit 1

Known-bad:
- Reconcile after reset_task_to_todo would see only terminal rows and falsely report complete
- Calling reconcile inside wave `outcome.terminal` would double-classify or downgrade authoritative codes
edgeCases: was_stopped=true with todo tasks remaining: exit 0 unchanged
exit_code already 1/2/130 from crash/blocked/signal: no downgrade
max iterations reached + count==0: upgrade to all tasks complete / Completed
deadline reached + count==0: exit 0, reason stays deadline reached

failureModes.cause: Reconcile called after step 17.5 resets in_progress to todo
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Must not happen — code review / grep gate; would mask incompleteness

failureModes.cause: count_remaining_active_tasks returns 0 on SQL error (existing unwrap_or 0)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Reconcile treats as drained; existing helper behavior unchanged in this task

userStories.notes: Design doc: docs/designs/batch-chain-incomplete-stop.md. Predicate: wave_scheduler.rs count_remaining_active_tasks (excludes done/irrelevant/skipped/blocked). Orchestrator default init at lines 133-134. Documented exit codes at orchestrator.rs:72-77. Learning [4324]: count_remaining_tasks is the consolidated SQL helper — do not duplicate. Learning [3927]: drained-queue vs stale-abort distinction lives in classify_drained_queue — reconcile is for budget/deadline fallthrough only. Learning [2695]: exit code consistency matters across paths — do not downgrade existing nonzero codes.
qualityDimensions: Single count_remaining_active_tasks read per reconcile call
Outer post-while placement only — no reconcile inside inner terminal paths
was_stopped and already-nonzero exit_code are early returns with zero mutation
Enriched exit reason includes N task(s) remaining for operator triage
Deadline+drained leaves deadline reached reason (one-line comment documents intentional asymmetry)
userStories.title: Add reconcile_ambiguous_exit + orchestrator post-loop wiring

acceptanceCriteria: LoopResult has pub prd_complete: bool field with doc comment; #[derive(Default)] yields false
orchestrator.rs normal return sets prd_complete from count_remaining_active_tasks == 0 after reconcile
batch.rs chain gate uses exit_code != 0 || !loop_result.prd_complete when chain=true
batch.rs emits Chain stopped: PRD did not complete, skipping remaining PRDs on chain break
batch.rs:740 synthetic LoopResult sets prd_complete: true
auto_review.rs passing_result sets prd_complete: true
engine.rs Default test asserts prd_complete == false
test_chain_stops_on_incomplete_prd exists: chain=true, exit_code=0, prd_complete=false → remaining PRDs have skipped=true
test_chain_false_incomplete_does_not_skip exists: chain=false, same incomplete signals (exit_code=1 or prd_complete=false) → downstream PRD skipped=false (batch continues)
test_stop_on_failure_results_structure covers exit_code=1 chain failure case (existing) plus documents chain=true incomplete skip invariants
Negative: chain=false path has no new behavior — grep confirms chain gate still wrapped in chain &&
Known-bad: chain gate checking only exit_code would let iteration-ceiling exit 0 advance chain before Layer 1 ships — prd_complete gate catches drift

CONTRACT + grep audit: batch reads loop_result.prd_complete on LoopResult from engine::run_loop; `rg 'LoopResult \{' --type rust` shows every literal sets prd_complete explicitly (batch.rs:740, auto_review.rs:454, orchestrator.rs:786) or uses Default/..Default::default() (startup.rs early returns); cargo check passes with zero missing-field errors
userStories.description:
Implement Layer 2: observable PRD completion flag and batch --chain stop when PRD did not complete.

DO (engine.rs):
- Add field to LoopResult (engine.rs ~893):
  `/// True when every task for this PRD prefix is in a terminal state (done/irrelevant/skipped/blocked) at loop end.`
  `pub prd_complete: bool,`
- Update engine.rs Default tests (~1214-1234) to assert prd_complete is false under LoopResult::default().

DO (orchestrator.rs normal return ~786):
- Set `prd_complete: count_remaining_active_tasks(&conn, task_prefix.as_deref()) == 0` AFTER reconcile_ambiguous_exit runs (same DB state reconcile used).

DO (batch.rs chain gate ~707):
- Replace `if chain && exit_code != 0` with chain break on `chain && (exit_code != 0 || !loop_result.prd_complete)`.
- Update message to: `Chain stopped: PRD did not complete, skipping remaining PRDs`.
- Do NOT advance chain_base when chain breaks (existing break before advancement already correct).

DO (call sites — grep LoopResult { and set explicitly where literals are constructed):
- batch.rs:740 synthetic LoopResult for auto-review → `prd_complete: true`
- auto_review.rs:454 passing_result helper → `prd_complete: true`
- startup.rs early-return LoopResult paths: rely on Default (false) — verify compile; add field only if struct literals need it
- Any test files constructing LoopResult { ... } without ..Default::default() — set prd_complete appropriately

DO (batch.rs tests):
- Add test_chain_stops_on_incomplete_prd: chain=true, exit_code=0, prd_complete=false → remaining PRDs skipped=true.
- Add test_chain_false_incomplete_does_not_skip: chain=false with exit_code=1 or prd_complete=false → downstream PRD skipped=false (regression guard for if chain && only).
- Keep test_stop_on_failure_results_structure for exit_code=1 chain failure; extend comments if needed for incomplete invariants.

DO NOT:
- Add prd_complete to PrdRunResult or batch summary output
- Change was_stopped early-break path in batch (lines 680-691)
- Touch chain=false code paths beyond the guarded `if chain &&` condition

Data contract (orchestrator → batch):
- Source: LoopResult { prd_complete: bool, exit_code: i32, ... } from engine::run_loop
- Consumer: batch.rs run_batch reads loop_result.prd_complete after await engine::run_loop
- Access: `let chain_break = chain && (exit_code != 0 || !loop_result.prd_complete);`
edgeCases: exit_code=0, prd_complete=false after iteration ceiling: chain stops
exit_code=1, prd_complete=true (hypothetical drift): chain still stops on exit_code
was_stopped=true: early batch break before chain gate — no behavior change
chain=false with incomplete PRD: batch continues to next PRD (unchanged)

failureModes.cause: Forgot to update a LoopResult struct literal after adding field
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Compiler error until all literals updated or use ..Default::default()
Would disagree with exit_code after reconcile — must compute after reconcile on same conn snapshot

userStories.notes: Depends on FEAT-001 reconcile running before prd_complete is computed (same post-reconcile DB snapshot). Validated: no `LoopResult` literals under tests/ — integration tests use run_loop return values only; compile gate is sufficient (do not add tests/e2e_loop.rs to touchesFiles). Grep: `rg 'LoopResult \{' --type rust` for literal updates in src/loop_engine/. Learning [4933]: batch --chain uses chain_base stacking — stopping chain on incomplete prevents downstream PRDs building on broken tips. Learning [3824]: auto-review must not change exit code — prd_complete on synthetic result is for truthfulness only. Learning [2716]: avoid duplicate exit_code checks — chain_break is one compound condition.
qualityDimensions: prd_complete computed from same count_remaining_active_tasks predicate as reconcile
Default false on early-return LoopResult paths (conservative)
Synthetic auto-review LoopResult prd_complete true reflects last successful complete PRD intent
Structure-only batch tests match batch-chain-worktrees.json style (no real run_loop spawn)
userStories.title: Add LoopResult.prd_complete + batch chain defensive gate
